code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by their subject
    subjects: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subjects.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Helper to turn a predicate/object into a clause fragment
    def phrase_for(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        mapping = {
            "author": f"written by {obj}",
            "isbnNumber": f"has ISBN {obj}",
            "language": f"is in {obj}",
            "mediaType": f"is a {obj}",
            "numberOfPages": f"has {obj} pages",
            "followedBy": f"is followed by {obj}",
            "precededBy": f"is preceded by {obj}",
            "publisher": f"published by {obj}",
            "publicationYear": f"was published in {obj}",
            "edition": f"is the {obj} edition",
            "genre": f"is a {obj} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"is a {obj} work",
            "releaseDate": f"was released on {obj}",
            "birthDate": f"was born on {obj}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {obj}",
            "birthPlace": f"was born in {obj}",
            "capital": f"has capital {obj}",
            "country": f"is from {obj}",
            "location": f"is located in {obj}",
            "largestCity": f"has its largest city {obj}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered in {obj}",
        }
        return mapping.get(pred, f"{pred} {obj}")

    sentences = []
    for subj, triples_list in subjects.items():
        # Start with the subject name
        clause_parts = []
        for t in triples_list:
            frag = phrase_for(t.predicate, t.object)
            clause_parts.append(frag)

        # Combine fragments: first fragment as main clause, rest as relative clauses
        if clause_parts:
            main = clause_parts[0]
            if len(clause_parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subj} {main}"
            else:
                rel = ", ".join(clause_parts[1:])
                sentence = f"{subj} {main}, which {rel}"
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join multiple subject sentences with a coordinating conjunction
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    parts = []
    for t in triples:
        pred = t.predicate
        # Simple natural‑language mapping for common predicates
        if pred == "author":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} was written by {t.object}"
        elif pred == "isbnNumber":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} has ISBN {t.object}"
        elif pred == "language":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} is in {t.object}"
        elif pred == "mediaType":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} is a {t.object}"
        elif pred == "numberOfPages":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} has {t.object} pages"
        elif pred == "followedBy":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} is followed by {t.object}"
        elif pred == "precededBy":
            phrase = f"{t.subject} is preceded by {t.object}"
        else:
            # Generic fallback
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}"
        parts.append(phrase)

    # Combine all parts into one sentence; use semicolons for readability
    if len(parts) == 1:
        return parts[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
